name: playwright
tagline: ClawHub (127★): Browser automation via Playwright MCP. Navigate websites, click elements, fill forms, take screenshots, extract data, and d
verdict: Quality and security are both strong here. The rough edges: the name field itself isn't kebab-case, the clawhub CLI it calls is never declared as a dependency, and there's no fallback advice if node/npx/Playwright are missing — despite the skill hard-depending on all three.

highlights.summary: name field is not kebab-case (spaces, parentheses, uppercase, plus signs); a separate slug field is kebab-case but the rubric checks name specifically.
Installs/invokes well-known, official packages (playwright, @playwright/mcp) from the standard npm registry, but without version pinning anywhere in the metadata or quick-start com
Flaky/CI test failures are addressed, but there is no guidance for what to do if node/npx, Playwright, or the MCP server are missing/unavailable despite being declared hard depende
